breaking_news.title: APT37 'Ruby Jumper' Campaign Breaches Air-Gapped Systems Using Zoho WorkDrive
breaking_news.summary:
North Korea-linked APT37 (ScarCruft) has launched a sophisticated campaign dubbed 'Ruby Jumper' targeting air-gapped networks. The group utilizes a novel attack chain involving malicious LNK files that deploy new implants like RESTLEAF and SNAKEDROPPER, leveraging Zoho WorkDrive for command-and-control and USB-based malware to bridge isolated systems.

This campaign represents a significant evolution in state-sponsored espionage, demonstrating how threat actors are combining cloud storage services with physical media to bypass traditional network isolation. Organizations with critical infrastructure or classified data relying on air-gapped security models are now at heightened risk of surveillance and data exfiltration.

breaking_news.title: Coruna iOS Exploit Kit with 23 Zero-Days Deployed in Espionage and Crypto Theft
breaking_news.summary:
Google Threat Intelligence Group identified 'Coruna,' a powerful spyware-grade exploit kit targeting iOS devices running versions 13.0 through 17.2.1. The kit contains five full exploit chains and 23 distinct vulnerabilities, including non-public techniques, and is currently being used by multiple actors for targeted espionage and cryptocurrency theft.

The widespread deployment of Coruna poses a severe threat to mobile users, as it enables attackers to bypass iOS security mitigations and gain full device control. This marks a significant escalation in mobile threat capabilities, affecting millions of unpatched devices and highlighting the proliferation of sophisticated zero-day arsenals in the wild.

breaking_news.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited VMware Aria Operations RCE Flaw to KEV Catalog
breaking_news.summary:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added CVE-2026-22719 to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog. This high-severity command injection flaw in Broadcom VMware Aria Operations allows unauthenticated attackers to achieve remote code execution, and it is currently being actively exploited in the wild.

Organizations relying on VMware Aria Operations for IT management face an immediate risk of full system compromise. The inclusion in the KEV catalog mandates urgent patching for federal agencies and serves as a critical warning for all enterprises to verify their patch levels to prevent unauthorized access and potential lateral movement.

breaking_news.title: OAuth Redirect Abuse Campaign Targets Government Entities with Malware
breaking_news.summary:
Microsoft has warned of a sophisticated phishing campaign abusing the OAuth protocol's legitimate redirection mechanism to bypass email and browser defenses. Attackers craft URLs using trusted identity providers like Microsoft Entra ID and Google Workspace to silently redirect victims to attacker-controlled infrastructure for malware delivery without stealing tokens.

This technique represents a significant evolution in identity-based attacks, rendering traditional URL filtering and link inspection ineffective. Government and public-sector organizations are primary targets, requiring immediate updates to security policies and user awareness training to mitigate the risk of credential compromise and malware infection.

breaking_news.title: APT28 Exploited MSHTML Zero-Day CVE-2026-21513 Prior to Patch Release
breaking_news.summary:
Russia-linked APT28 (Fancy Bear) is confirmed to have exploited a high-severity zero-day vulnerability, CVE-2026-21513, in the MSHTML framework before Microsoft patched it in February 2026. The flaw allows attackers to bypass security controls and execute arbitrary code by tricking victims into opening malicious HTML or LNK files.

The active exploitation of this zero-day by a known state-sponsored group highlights the persistent threat to Windows environments. Organizations must ensure all systems are updated to the latest patch levels immediately, as unpatched systems remain vulnerable to remote code execution and potential data theft.

breaking_news.title: Qualcomm Zero-Day CVE-2026-21385 Actively Exploited in Targeted Android Attacks
breaking_news.summary:
Google has confirmed that a high-severity vulnerability in a Qualcomm graphics component, CVE-2026-21385, is being actively exploited in targeted attacks against Android devices. The flaw involves an integer overflow leading to memory corruption, allowing attackers to access sensitive memory data and potentially execute arbitrary code.

This zero-day exploitation affects a wide range of Android devices running unpatched versions of the Qualcomm component. Users are urged to update their devices immediately to the March 2026 security bulletin or later to mitigate the risk of compromise, as the attacks appear to be limited but highly targeted.

breaking_news.title: CyberStrikeAI Open-Source Tool Used in Mass FortiGate Firewall Breaches
breaking_news.summary:
Researchers have identified a new open-source AI-native platform, CyberStrikeAI, being used by threat actors to automate attacks against Fortinet FortiGate firewalls. The tool, which packages over 100 curated attack tools into a single orchestration engine, was linked to a campaign that breached hundreds of firewalls across 55 countries.

The emergence of AI-driven attack automation lowers the barrier to entry for sophisticated cyberattacks, enabling even novice actors to launch complex campaigns. Organizations deploying FortiGate appliances must review their configurations and monitor for signs of compromise, as this toolset represents a significant shift in the threat landscape.

breaking_news.title: Chrome Vulnerability CVE-2026-0628 Allowed Extensions to Hijack Gemini AI Panel
breaking_news.summary:
A high-severity vulnerability in Google Chrome, CVE-2026-0628, allowed malicious extensions to hijack the Gemini Live AI assistant panel. This flaw enabled low-privilege extensions to escalate privileges, gaining access to local files, screenshots, and camera/microphone controls through the browser's embedded AI feature.

Although patched in January 2026, the discovery underscores the risks associated with agentic AI features in browsers and the potential for extension abuse. Users must ensure their Chrome browsers are fully updated to prevent similar privilege escalation attacks that could lead to severe privacy violations and data theft.

breaking_news.title: University of Hawaii Cancer Center Ransomware Attack Exposes 1.2 Million Records
breaking_news.summary:
The University of Hawaii Cancer Center confirmed a massive ransomware attack affecting 1.24 million individuals, with sensitive data including Social Security numbers and historical voter records compromised. The attack, which occurred in August 2025, targeted the Epidemiology Division's research servers.

This breach highlights the ongoing vulnerability of healthcare and research institutions to ransomware gangs. The exposure of historical voter records and personal health information poses significant long-term risks for identity theft and fraud for the affected individuals, necessitating immediate notification and remediation efforts.

breaking_news.title: Iranian Drone Strikes Damage AWS Data Centers in UAE and Bahrain
breaking_news.summary:
Amazon Web Services confirmed that drone strikes in the Middle East have damaged three data centers in the UAE and one in Bahrain, causing extensive outages for cloud services. This physical attack on critical cloud infrastructure marks a rare instance of kinetic warfare directly impacting digital service availability.

The incident underscores the vulnerability of cloud infrastructure to geopolitical conflicts and physical attacks. Organizations relying on AWS services in the Middle East region face immediate service disruptions and must evaluate their disaster recovery and business continuity plans to mitigate the impact of such physical threats.
